title: A-Admission pre-execution rejection contract
description: Package-level extension for pre-execution admission gates in the strategy manual. A-Admission extends the framework lemma-schema alphabet to operationalize the both-sides test, bookkeeping-vs-theorem test, defect-register entry, and execution rejection rules specified in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, runtime model and repair protocol).

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `A-Admission`.

invocationRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the exact tactic/node invocation, payload handoff, loop pass, or certificate-validation attempt that was rejected.

failedConditions.description: Required nonempty list of admission predicates that are false, missing, ill typed, or unsupported. Each entry must state the expected condition and observed defect, such as absent H_0/E/I/R/V/Q/A data, wrong payload arity or sort, missing typed consumer route, unproved S-Comp comparison, missing ledger payer/account, missing bounded residual on the negative side, or an undeclared global theorem.

items.description: One precisely stated failed precondition for the rejected invocation.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the required condition and why the current state does not establish it.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the failed mathematical predicate or type judgment.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal/type-checking language in which failure was detected.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable failed obligation, diagnostic judgment, or counterexample.

defectRegisterRef.description: Required nonempty identifier of the defect-register entry that owns this rejection and records its status, dependencies, and resolution history.

remediation.description: Optional concrete next action needed before the invocation can be retried, such as discharging a named S-contract, adding a missing typed field to V, proving S-Pers persistence, recomputing a finite table, fixing a P_{i->j} route/signature mismatch, supplying both sides of the admission gate, or sending a genuine non-expressibility issue to A-Scope.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of audit, type-checking, proof, calculation, or review sources establishing the failed admission conditions.

items.description: One evidence record supporting pre-execution rejection.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet; select the category matching the failed check or diagnostic.

reference.description: Required nonempty stable locator for the invocation signature, failed contract, validation report, defect row, or mathematical countercheck.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a generated validation report, input payload, table, or formal diagnostic.

notes.description: Optional note mapping this source to one or more failed conditions and explaining why execution was not permitted.
